Video Encoder uses two
containers based on the protocol used:

- along with this,
video encoder will compress
the video into multiple-bitrates
(480p, 720p, 1080p, 4k)

to handle
the chunks
based on client's
internet bandwidth

The whole 
large video will be
splitted in chunks
(1-6 sec, 7-12 sec etc)

From where this search 
data is coming???

Response: Metadata of that video
(before clicking on PLAY button)

This Manifest 

file is stored
as metadata of the
video

Posting this small

compressed format again into S3 Blob-storage

Comment
- comment_id
- post_id
- user_id
- like_count
- other data

Like DB
- like_id
post_id / comment_id
- user_id
- reaction_type
- other data

Get me 
all recent video 
based on my
subscriber

Video (Document DB)
- Video_id
- user_id
- video_type
- content_text
- media_url
- thumbnail_url
- like_count
-share_count
- comment_ount
- other meta data
After seeing metadata of video, if user likes the video, he will click on PLAY button
S3 Blobl storage will return the requested video file
2. Get the list of all subscribers to backfill

We can use AI-ML 
models to see the best
recommendation
for top models
